title: Development and optimization of a new culture media using extruded bean as nitrogen source
author: Batista, Fernandes

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Briefly, the development of the new culture media containing extruded bean as substitute of traditional nitrogen source (peptone and yeast extract) involved the following steps:(1)Production of the extruded bean flour.(2)Use of statistical approach to optimize the composition of nitrogen source.(3)Evaluation of the yeast growth profile in the new media compared to a commercial YPD media.(4)Estimation of the potential use of the new culture media for heterologous expression.

steps.title: Media preparation
steps.instruction: The preparation of culture media containing extruded bean as nitrogen source was carried out considering the percentage of substitution determined by the mathematical design. Considering that extruded bean is a complex material not completely soluble, after dissolution of all components and before autoclaving, the medium was filtered through a cellulosic membrane (gauze) to avoid the presence of fibrous insoluble materials.

steps.title: Statistical analysis
steps.instruction: The statistical analysis of the experimental mixture design was performed by multivariate analysis. The model was simplified to exclude terms that were not considered statistically significant (p > 0.05) by analysis of variance (ANOVA). The quality of the polynomial model equation was judged by using the coefficient of determination r2.

steps.title: Statistical analysis
steps.instruction: The response function (Y) of the mixture model was explained by the following equation:(1)y=∑i=1qβixi+∑∑i<jqβijxixj

steps.title: Statistical analysis
steps.instruction: Geometrically, in Eq. (1) the parameter βi represents the expected response of the pure mixture xi = 1, xj = 0, j ≠ i. The term given by y=∑i=1qβixi represents the response when blending is strictly additives and there are no interactions between the components of the mixture. The quadratic term βijxixj represents the excess response over the linear model due to interaction between two components [11].

steps.title: Statistical analysis
steps.instruction: The mixture design and all subsequent tests were conducted in triplicate and the level of significance was 95%. All analyses were carried out by using the software Statistica 7.0 (StatSoft Inc., Tulsa, OK, USA).
